summarization.a: Scatter plot of Ar⁺ sputter etch depth vs acceleration grid voltage (50 s/cycle × 100 cycles). Etching is negligible up to 10 V, becomes measurable by ~30 V, and increases sharply by 50 V, indicating a strong voltage/ion-energy dependence.
summarization.b: Multiple scatter plot showing InGaAs etch depth as a function of Ar⁺ ion exposure time at acceleration grid voltages of 10 V and 30 V. The 30 V condition produces substantially higher etch depths over the same exposure times, indicating a strong dependence of sputter yield on ion energy.

b.question: Does the etch depth at 30 V exceed that at 10 V for the non-zero Ar⁺ exposure times shown in subfigure (b)?
In subfigure (b), describe how etch depth depends on Ar⁺ exposure time for 10 V vs 30 V. What does this imply about sputtering conditions?
b.answer: Etch depth increases non-linearly with exposure time for both, but 30 V increases much faster (≈20 → 95 → 210 Å) while 10 V stays ~0 up to 100 s and only rises by 150 s (~75 Å). This implies sputtering is strongly ion-energy dependent; at lower voltage there’s an effective “low-yield” regime over much of the time range.

a.question: In subfigure (a), at approximately what acceleration grid voltage does the etch depth first become clearly measurable (non-zero)?
In subfigure (a), what fixed Ar⁺ ion exposure condition is stated in the inset text?
